market.advice: US session closed with the tape still healthy: VIX calm at 16.8, zero distribution days in the last 25 sessions, SPY ($745) holding above its 50-day ($738). But breadth is only moderate — 63% of stocks above their 50-day, not the 80%+ you see at a thrust — and there's been no confirmed follow-through day yet. Read: this is a market to be selectively aggressive in, not blindly aggressive in. Favor A+ VCP setups with tight, defined risk (sub-8% stops) over chasing names already extended 20%+ off a base. Normal position sizing on the STRONG BUY tier; size down or wait for a pullback on the WATCH tier.

picks.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
picks.thesis: Tightest VCP in the custodial-bank group (3 contractions, 2.5% tightest pullback) with the RS line at new highs and EPS +42%/revenue +13% YoY backing it — earnings hit in 7 days, the classic pre-report catalyst window. TradingView's technicals agree (14 buy / 3 sell / 9 neutral), reinforcing the breakout thesis.
strengths: Confirmed Stage 2 uptrend, price above rising MA50 and MA200
RS line at new highs vs S&P — institutions leading, not lagging
risks: Earnings in 7 days — binary event risk into the print
Financials as a group are already crowded on this scan

picks.name: Northern Trust
picks.thesis: Near-identical setup to BK — 3-contraction VCP, RS at new highs, EPS +43%/revenue +14% — but with earnings 14 days out, giving more room to work before the print. TV rating (14/3/9) confirms.
strengths: RS line at new highs vs S&P 500
EPS +43%, revenue +14% YoY — best fundamentals in the trust-bank space
risks: Trades in lockstep with BK/STT — not a diversifier if you already hold one

picks.name: PNC Financial Services
picks.thesis: Regional-bank leader with a confirmed VCP and RSI still mid-range (65.5) — room left before overbought. TV agrees (12 buy / 4 sell / 10 neutral); earnings in 7 days is the near-term catalyst.
strengths: RSI 65.5 — not yet overbought, room to run
risks: Earnings in 7 days
-3% off 52-week high — hasn't broken out yet, still base-building

picks.name: JPMorgan Chase
picks.thesis: The mega-cap anchor for the financials theme — RSI a healthy 50.8 (least extended of the group), VCP confirmed, TV agrees (11/5/10). Earnings in 6 days.
strengths: RSI 50.8 — most room to run of any bank pick here
risks: Earnings in 6 days
Not yet at RS highs — lagging the strongest regionals

picks.name: Liquidia Corporation
picks.thesis: Highest score in the entire scan. Explosive 108% 3-month momentum, RS at new highs, volume dried up to 56% of average (sellers exhausted) ahead of a tight 5-contraction VCP. TV confirms (14/5/7) and revenue growth is enormous (+4,159% YoY off a tiny base) as its pulmonary hypertension drug ramps.
strengths: RS line at new highs — institutional accumulation
Volume dried up to 56% of average — sellers gone
risks: EPS still negative — early-stage biotech, no earnings support yet
RSI 72.4 — approaching overbought, size or scale in rather than chase

picks.name: Eli Lilly
picks.thesis: The GLP-1 leader keeps compounding — EPS +170%, revenue +56% YoY, RS at new highs, tight 5-contraction VCP. TV agrees (14/3/9) despite a recent negative news blip, which is the one thing to watch.
strengths: EPS +170%, revenue +56% YoY — best fundamentals of any large-cap pick here
risks: Recent news flow slightly negative — monitor for a sentiment-driven gap
High absolute share price ($1,216) — position-size in dollar terms, not shares

picks.name: Elevance Health
picks.thesis: Managed-care name with RS at new highs and RSI a moderate 59 — un-extended relative to peers. TV strongly agrees (14/2/10). Earnings in 7 days is the catalyst to watch.
strengths: RS at new highs, RSI only 59 — not overbought
TV rating 14 buy / 2 sell — one of the cleanest confirmations in this list
risks: EPS -17% YoY — earnings quality lags the technical setup
Earnings in 7 days — binary risk into the print

picks.name: CVS Health
picks.thesis: Turnaround story confirmed technically — RS at new highs, EPS +63% YoY off depressed comps, RSI 61 with room. TV agrees strongly (14/2/10).
risks: Earnings in 28 days — less immediate catalyst than peers
Comps still easy off a depressed base — watch for deceleration

picks.name: Fortinet
picks.thesis: Cybersecurity leader with RS at new highs, EPS +29%/revenue +20% YoY, and a tight 4-contraction VCP. TV agrees (13/3/10) with no earnings event for 22 days — cleaner runway than the bank picks.
strengths: RS at new highs — leading its sector
No earnings for 22 days — clean technical runway
risks: Forward P/E 45.7 — priced for continued growth, vulnerable to any deceleration

picks.name: Dell Technologies
picks.thesis: AI-server demand is showing up in the numbers — EPS +283%, revenue +88% YoY — with 134% 3-month momentum. TV confirms decisively (15 buy / 1 sell / 10 neutral).
strengths: EPS +283%, revenue +88% YoY — the strongest fundamental print in this list
risks: -7% off 52-week high, RS not yet confirmed at new highs — lagging the pure AI-chip names slightly
Momentum this strong can mean-revert sharply on any guide-down

picks.name: Taiwan Semiconductor
picks.thesis: The foundry behind every AI chip — EPS +58%, revenue +35% YoY — with RSI at a moderate 52.5, meaning it hasn't run yet. TV agrees (11/6/9). Earnings in 8 days is the catalyst.
strengths: RSI 52.5 — least extended of the semiconductor picks
Structurally the best-positioned name in AI infrastructure
risks: Earnings in 8 days — event risk plus Taiwan geopolitical overhang
-8.6% off 52-week high — needs to reclaim highs to confirm leadership

picks.name: First Industrial Realty Trust
picks.thesis: Industrial-warehouse REIT with a textbook 5-contraction VCP, volume surging 1.6x average, and EPS +200% YoY. TV confirms strongly (15/2/9).
strengths: Volume surge 1.6x average — institutional demand showing up now
risks: RS line -5% off its high — underperforming the broader market somewhat despite the strong base

picks.name: Innovative Industrial Properties
picks.thesis: Cannabis-industry REIT with RS at new highs and volume dried up to 54% of average — sellers exhausted ahead of a tight base. TV agrees (14/2/10). Earnings already passed, removing near-term binary risk.
strengths: RS at new highs vs S&P 500
Volume dried up to 54% — stock resting, not distributing
Earnings already passed — no report risk before the setup plays out
risks: EPS and revenue both roughly flat YoY — technical setup is ahead of the fundamentals
RSI 73.7 — approaching overbought

picks.name: Terreno Realty
picks.thesis: Coastal-market industrial REIT with EPS +40%/revenue +13% YoY and RSI a calm 57.2. TV agrees (14/5/7).
strengths: RSI 57.2 — one of the least-extended picks in this whole list
risks: Only 6.2% 3-month momentum — slower mover than the other REIT picks, needs patience

picks.name: Howmet Aerospace
picks.thesis: Aerospace-parts supplier riding the commercial-jet build cycle — EPS +71%, revenue +19% YoY — with RSI only 43.7, among the least extended picks on this list. TV agrees (13/4/9).
strengths: RSI 43.7 — least overbought pick in the whole set
risks: -4.1% off high but hasn't confirmed RS at new highs yet — still needs to prove leadership
Earnings in 29 days

picks.name: Smith & Wesson Brands
picks.thesis: Small-cap firearms maker with EPS +87%/revenue +27% YoY and TV's strongest confirmation in this list (16 buy / 2 sell). Lower score reflects small size and -7% distance off high, not setup quality.
strengths: TV rating 16/2/8 — the most lopsided bullish TV read of any pick here
risks: Small-cap, low liquidity relative to mega-caps on this list — size positions accordingly
Earnings not until 58 days out — long wait for a catalyst

picks.name: Werner Enterprises
picks.thesis: Trucking name showing 37% 3-month momentum on a confirmed VCP. TV agrees (13/3/10), suggesting freight-cycle recovery is starting to get priced in.
strengths: 36.8% 3-month momentum — one of the strongest movers in the industrials group
risks: EPS growth not disclosed/unclear in this scan — verify the earnings trend before sizing up
Freight cycle is historically volatile; confirm with sector peers (e.g. rails) before adding

picks.name: Starbucks
picks.thesis: Turnaround narrative backed by numbers — EPS +33%, revenue +9% YoY — and TV's cleanest read in the consumer group (15 buy / 1 sell).
strengths: EPS +33%, revenue +9% YoY — turnaround plan showing in results
risks: RSI 56.5 and only -2.8% off high but 3-month momentum a modest 7.5% — this is a grinder, not a breakout, size expectations accordingly

picks.name: Monster Beverage
picks.thesis: Defensive-growth hybrid — EPS +29%, revenue +27% YoY — with 27% 3-month momentum. TV agrees (11/5/10).
strengths: EPS +29%, revenue +27% YoY — rare combination of defensive category and real growth
risks: RS not yet confirmed at new highs — hasn't proven full sector leadership

picks.name: Altria Group
picks.thesis: Classic defensive-income name with EPS +106% YoY and TradingView's single strongest technical read of any pick in this whole list (STRONG_BUY, 16 buy / 1 sell).
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY rating, 16 buy / 1 sell — best TV alignment on this list
Low beta — ballast if the broader tape wobbles
risks: Only 10.6% 3-month momentum — this is a slow, steady mover, not a fast breakout
Structural volume-decline risk long-term to the core tobacco business

picks.name: Kinder Morgan
picks.thesis: Midstream energy pick with EPS +36%/revenue +14% YoY and TV agreement (14/2/10). Earnings in 8 days.
strengths: Pipeline/midstream model is less commodity-price-sensitive than E&P
risks: 3-month momentum slightly negative (-0.6%) — base is still forming, not yet breaking out
Earnings in 8 days

picks.name: EOG Resources
picks.thesis: E&P name with EPS +40%/revenue +16% YoY and TradingView's rare STRONG_BUY rating (16/1/9) — the same top TV tier as MO, giving this the best technical-fundamental alignment in the energy sector pick.
strengths: Diversifies the STRONG BUY list into commodity/E&P exposure
risks: -7.5% off 52-week high, flat 3-month momentum — needs oil-price cooperation to break out
Earnings in 28 days

picks.name: Match Group
picks.thesis: Dating-app portfolio (Tinder/Hinge) turning the corner — EPS +52% YoY — with TV agreement (14/3/9) and a cheap forward P/E of 9.1 relative to growth.
strengths: Forward P/E just 9.1 — cheapest valuation of any STRONG BUY pick on this list
risks: Revenue growth a modest 3.9% YoY — earnings growth is coming more from margin than top line
Earnings in 28 days

picks.name: SiriusXM Holdings
picks.thesis: Satellite-radio name with RS at new highs and TV agreement (14/4/8), but RSI at 78.6 means it's already extended — chase with a tighter stop or wait for a pullback toward the 21-day.
risks: RSI 78.6 — extended, elevated odds of a near-term pullback
No VCP base confirmed by the scanner — this is a momentum extension, not a fresh breakout

picks.name: DTE Energy
picks.thesis: Utility pick offering ballast with a confirmed VCP and TV agreement (12/4/10) — a lower-beta complement to the tech/financials names above.
risks: EPS growth reported at -44% YoY in this scan — verify against one-time items before sizing; utility earnings can be lumpy around rate cases
Only 2.6% 3-month momentum — slow mover

picks.name: Entergy
picks.thesis: Second utility pick for diversification and AI-datacenter power-demand exposure (Entergy serves several hyperscaler build-outs in Louisiana/Mississippi). TV agrees (14/2/10).
strengths: TV 14 buy / 2 sell — one of the cleanest utility reads
Direct beneficiary of AI-driven power demand growth
risks: EPS growth essentially flat (+1.2%) — the AI-power thesis is a 2027+ story, not fully in current earnings
Rate-case/regulatory risk typical of the utility sector

picks.name: Okta
picks.thesis: Identity-security leader with a genuinely excellent VCP (RS at new highs, EPS +21%/revenue +11% YoY) but RSI at 84.4 is deep overbought — TV still likes it (14/6/6) but the technical setup argues for a pullback entry rather than chasing here.
strengths: RS at new highs, EPS +21%/revenue +11% YoY
TV still bullish despite the extension (14/6/6)
risks: RSI 84.4 — the most overbought pick in this entire list
Chasing here risks buying the top of the near-term move; wait for a flag or pullback to the rising MA20

picks.name: Progyny
picks.thesis: Fertility-benefits name with 82% 3-month momentum and RS at new highs — a real leader — but RSI 87.1 is the single most overbought reading in the whole scan. TV still confirms (14/5/7); treat as a watch-for-pullback, not a chase.
strengths: 81.7% 3-month momentum — one of the fastest movers on this list
risks: RSI 87.1 — most overbought name in the entire universe scanned
Revenue growth only 1.4% YoY — the move is sentiment/momentum-driven more than fundamentals-driven right now

picks.name: Advanced Micro Devices
picks.thesis: The scan likes AMD (EPS +91%, revenue +38% YoY, 123% 3-month momentum) but TradingView rates it NEUTRAL (8 buy / 8 sell / 10 neutral) — a genuine split verdict. Held by one user; treat as hold-and-watch rather than an aggressive add until TV's oscillators confirm.
strengths: 123% 3-month momentum — one of the fastest movers in the scan
risks: TV rates NEUTRAL (8/8/10) — directly contradicts the scan's STRONG BUY signal
-10.9% off 52-week high despite the momentum — hasn't reclaimed leadership
Momentum this extreme after such a run raises mean-reversion risk

picks.name: GE Aerospace
picks.thesis: Strong scan score (revenue +25% YoY, confirmed VCP) but TV rates it NEUTRAL (8/8/10) and EPS is actually down 1.8% YoY — the fundamentals lag the technical picture. Earnings in 8 days adds event risk on top of the mixed read.
strengths: Revenue +25% YoY — aerospace demand cycle intact
risks: TV NEUTRAL (8/8/10) — no technical confirmation of the scan's STRONG BUY call
EPS -1.8% YoY — earnings quality doesn't match the price action
Earnings in 8 days

picks.name: Ralph Lauren
picks.thesis: The scan's model likes the fundamentals (EPS +20%, revenue +17% YoY) but this is the sharpest scan-vs-TV split on the entire list: TV rates it an outright SELL (6 buy / 11 sell / 9 neutral) with RSI at 31.9 (oversold, not a breakout) and -8% off the 52-week high. This reads as a value/mean-reversion candidate, not a momentum breakout — treat very differently from the STRONG BUY tier above.
strengths: RSI 31.9 — oversold, so downside may be limited from here for value buyers
risks: TV rates outright SELL (6/11/9) — the sharpest scan/TV disagreement in this entire report
-8% off high with negative RS — currently a laggard, not a leader
Consumer discretionary/apparel is more macro-sensitive than the other picks here
